title: Capture Toward the Center
setup: White has played **d4**, offering a pawn trade. Black takes — and grabs the center.
hint: Always recapture toward the center when you have a choice — it improves pawn structure.
explanation:
## The Idea

This is a basic **central pawn capture** — the foundational move from which the famous **Center Game** and many open positions arise.

Black plays {{exd4}}, capturing the d-pawn with the e-pawn.

## Why This Capture?

- The black pawn moves from **e5** (a less central square) to **d4** (more central).
- White's central pawn is gone, freeing Black's pieces.
- Material is now even, but Black has improved pawn structure.

## What If Black Doesn't Take?

If Black ignores the offer:

- White could play {{dxe5}} on the next move, winning the e-pawn.
- Or White could play {{d5}}, gaining a strong wedge.

Either way Black loses central influence.

## Pattern to Memorize

> When you can capture **toward the center**, almost always do so. Centralized pawns control more squares, support more pieces, and create more threats.

The simple act of playing exd4 here represents a major principle: **fight for the center with every move you can.**
